dustry campaign, to be carried on
in conjunction with the chamber OF
commerce; allied itself with the
Asiatic Exclusion league, and


named delegates to the trades


council. The officers elected fol.
low:


President, A. w. Swenson; vice


president, G. J.. Bennington; secre.
tary. Samuel Kennedy; executive
committee. A. w. Nolan, Harry
Hayes, J. r. Morford, H. H. George;


sergeant at arms, P. Shaefer; read
Ing clerk, F. Barker.
Delegates to the trades council:
George, Barker, Swenson and Gar


rison.


Delegate to the Washington State
Federation of Labor convention, w.
ID. Wheaton.


One apprentice, Clayton Far
quhar, of The Press composing


room, was initiated, and five others
accepted. The Spokane union num-
bers over 100 members and has


made highly satisfactory progress
during the past year Its jurisdic.


ton 'extends over all typographical
affairs of the city and its prospect


for the future is strong in every
particular.
